success.description: Indicates whether the charge was created successfully

chargeId.description: Unique identifier of the created charge

message.description: Error message describing the bad request

parameters.name: meterId
parameters.description: Billing Meter ID (you can find this on your app's pricing page on the developer portal)

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Filter results by User ID that your server passed via API when the charge was created

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Filter results AFTER a specific date. Use this in combination with endDate to filter results in a specific time window.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Filter results BEFORE a specific date. Use this in combination with startDate to filter results in a specific time window.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of records to return

refunded.description: Value is 'true' if the charge has subsequently been refunded.

currency.description: Currency of the transaction. We currently support USD only.

meterId.description: Billing Meter ID (you can find this on your app's pricing page)

entityType.description: Indicates who was charged? Currently, we support charges for 'location' only

entityId.description: If the entityType is Location, entityld would be locationld.

pricePerUnit.description: Price per unit for the charge

transactionType.description: This can be one of two values - 'charge' or 'refund'

units.description: Number of units that the sub-account was charged for

meta.description: meta object contains details that were sent while creating the charge via the API - eventID, description, eventTime, userld

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the charge was created in our system

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the charge was last updated in our system

pagination.description: Pagination metadata for the charges list

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return

parameters.name: chargeId
parameters.description: ID of the charge to delete

success.description: Indicates whether the charge was deleted successfully

message.description: Error message describing why the charge was not found

parameters.name: chargeId
parameters.description: ID of the charge to retrieve

refunded.description: Value is 'true' if the charge has subsequently been refunded.

currency.description: Currency of the transaction. We currently support USD only.

meterId.description: Billing Meter ID (you can find this on your app's pricing page)

entityType.description: Indicates who was charged? Currently, we support charges for 'location' only

entityId.description: If the entityType is Location, entityld would be locationld.

pricePerUnit.description: Price per unit for the charge

transactionType.description: This can be one of two values - 'charge' or 'refund'

units.description: Number of units that the sub-account was charged for

meta.description: meta object contains details that were sent while creating the charge via the API - eventID, description, eventTime, userld

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the charge was created in our system

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the charge was last updated in our system

message.description: Error message describing why the charge was not found

get.summary: Check if account has sufficient funds
get.description: Check if account has sufficient funds

hasFunds.description: Indicates whether the sub-account has sufficient funds to be charged

delete.description: Uninstalls an application from your company or a specific location. This will remove the application`s access and stop all its functionalities

parameters.name: appId
parameters.description: The application id which is to be uninstalled.

get.description: Fetches installer details for the authenticated user. This endpoint returns information about the company, location, user, and installation details associated with the current OAuth token.

parameters.name: appId
parameters.description: ID of the app to get installer details

200.description: Successfully retrieved installer details. Returns company, location, user, and installation information.

400.description: Bad Request. Invalid request parameters or missing required data.

403.description: Forbidden. The client does not have necessary permissions to access installer details.

get.summary: Get rebilling config for an app subscription and usage plans
get.description: Get rebilling config for an app subscription and usage plans for the authenticated sub-account. This endpoint returns the subscription and usage plans for an app.

parameters.name: appId
parameters.description: ID of the app to get rebilling config

parameters.name: locationId
parameters.description: ID of the Sub-Account location to get rebilling config for

200.description: Successfully retrieved rebilling config for the app

400.description: Bad Request. Invalid request parameters or missing required data.

403.description: Forbidden. The client does not have necessary permissions to access installer details.

post.description: Migrates an external authentication connection credentials (basic or oauth2) for a specific app and location. This endpoint validates the app configuration, stores credentials safely in CRM's native encrypted storage. With this the lifecycle of the token is managed by CRM.

400.description: Bad request - invalid input or auth type mismatch

bearer.name: Authorization
bearer.description: Use the Access Token generated with user type as Sub-Account (OR) Private Integration Token of Sub-Account.

Location-Access.name: Authorization
Location-Access.description: Use the Access Token generated with user type as Sub-Account (OR) Private Integration Token of Sub-Account.

Location-Access-Only.name: Authorization
Location-Access-Only.description: Use the Access Token generated with user type as Sub-Account.

Agency-Access.name: Authorization
Agency-Access.description: Use the Access Token generated with user type as Agency (OR) Private Integration Token of Agency.

Agency-Access-Only.name: Authorization
Agency-Access-Only.description: Use the Access Token generated with user type as Agency.

meterId.description: Billing Meter ID (you can find this on your app's pricing page)

eventId.description: Event ID / Transaction ID on your server's side. This will help you maintain the reference of the event/transaction on your end that you charged the customer for.

locationId.description: ID of the Sub-Account to be charged

companyId.description: ID of the Agency the Sub-account belongs to

eventTime.description: The timestamp when the event/transaction was performed. If blank, the billing timestamp will be set as the event time. ISO8601 Format.

companyId.description: The company id from which the application is to be uninstalled. If you pass agency token, then companyId is required. It will uninstall application from agency as well as all sub-accounts.

locationId.description: The location id from which the application is to be uninstalled. If you pass location token, then locationId is required. It will uninstall application from that location only.

reason.description: The reason for uninstalling the application. Reason is required if you are uninstalling the application as a developer.
reason.example: Application is not working as expected

success.description: The status of the uninstallation of the application

logoUrl.description: Logo URL of the whitelabel company

companyEmail.description: Company email. Will be null for sub-account installations due to PII concerns.

companyOwnerFullName.description: Company owner full name. Will be null for sub-account installations due to PII concerns.

userId.description: User ID who installed the app

isWhitelabelCompany.description: Whether the company is a whitelabel company

companyPlan.description: Company plan. Will be null for sub-account installations due to business sensitivity.

companyHighLevelPlan.description: Company plan. Will be null for sub-account installations due to business sensitivity.

marketplaceAppPlanId.description: Marketplace app plan ID for paid apps

whitelabelDetails.description: Whitelabel details (only present if isWhitelabelCompany is true)

usageUnit.description: The usage unit for the meter

fixedPricePerUnit.description: The fixed price per unit, applicable for fixed price type

minPricePerUnit.description: The min price per unit, applicable for dynamic price type

maxPricePerUnit.description: The max price per unit, applicable for dynamic price type

type.description: Type of authentication - basic or oauth2

apiKey.description: API Key (supported when type is basic)

basicCredentials.description: Basic auth credentials as key/value pairs (supported when type is basic). Keys are validated against the app version externalAuthConfig.fields.

accessToken.description: Access token (required when type is oauth2)

refreshToken.description: Refresh token (required when type is oauth2)

expiryIn.description: Access token expiry time in milliseconds (optional for oauth2)

expiryAt.description: Timestamp for access token expiry (optional for oauth2)

displayName.description: Display name for the connection (optional, defaults to accountId)

isDefault.description: Whether this is the default connection for the location (optional, defaults to false)

success.description: Indicates if the migration was successful

identifier.description: Unique identifier for the migrated connection

message.description: Error message describing the internal server error
